---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 0059e93b2b9c71eacaf6a1e6596a02f60a93fb90
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc3-entry-vs-root-selector, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The story argues --root is mermaid-render-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space, but this reinterprets what the parent decision ratified the scope field to BE — proposals will carry a scope value whose declared semantics (--root) differ from the mechanism actually used to regenerate truth (--entry). The deviation is disclosed in dc-3's prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6 is declared, and the claim that the two flags share an identical selector value space is asserted, not established by the parent decision. (confidence 0.65)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-dc2-ambiguity-widens-partial-coverage, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-3 defines the three coverage values by generator vocabulary alone: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. Story dc-2 (with dc-1) adds a new, undeclared trigger for partial: a flowchart entirely WITHIN the declared grammar still falls to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides and a proposal node becomes ambiguous. This widens the parent's ratified meaning of 'partial' from a vocabulary property of the artifact's text to also cover an identity-resolution property of the truth graph, without a supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-3 — consumers of the coverage field per the parent's definition would misread ambiguity-induced partial as a vocabulary gap. (confidence 0.50)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/verification-extractor#dc-1, spec/verification-extractor#dc-2, spec/verification-extractor#dc-3, spec/verification-extractor#dc-4, spec/verification-extractor#dc-5] }
digest: sha256:b50e93cc1bde578329a8f62652e0b3581ac6d24719cb6166765269c3db7dd90d
integrity: sha256:d7e5cfa54b1fd19f8d8d365c04cd4b61c2d17826065a14e909a1d062657eb427
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dc3-entry-vs-root-selector\",\"text\":\"Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The story argues --root is mermaid-render-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space, but this reinterprets what the parent decision ratified the scope field to BE — proposals will carry a scope value whose declared semantics (--root) differ from the mechanism actually used to regenerate truth (--entry). The deviation is disclosed in dc-3's prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6 is declared, and the claim that the two flags share an identical selector value space is asserted, not established by the parent decision.\",\"confidence\":0.65,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"dc2-ambiguity-widens-partial-coverage\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-3 defines the three coverage values by generator vocabulary alone: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. Story dc-2 (with dc-1) adds a new, undeclared trigger for partial: a flowchart entirely WITHIN the declared grammar still falls to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides and a proposal node becomes ambiguous. This widens the parent's ratified meaning of 'partial' from a vocabulary property of the artifact's text to also cover an identity-resolution property of the truth graph, without a supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-3 — consumers of the coverage field per the parent's definition would misread ambiguity-induced partial as a vocabulary gap.\",\"confidence\":0.5,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc3-entry-vs-root-selector** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 substitutes flowmap's --entry flag for the --root selector that parent dc-6 pins as the meaning of the scope field ('the flowmap --root selector it was generated under'). The story argues --root is mermaid-render-only and --entry is the JSON-mode equivalent over the same selector value space, but this reinterprets what the parent decision ratified the scope field to BE — proposals will carry a scope value whose declared semantics (--root) differ from the mechanism actually used to regenerate truth (--entry). The deviation is disclosed in dc-3's prose but no supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-6 is declared, and the claim that the two flags share an identical selector value space is asserted, not established by the parent decision. (confidence 0.65)
- **judged-dc2-ambiguity-widens-partial-coverage** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Parent dc-3 defines the three coverage values by generator vocabulary alone: full means 'flowchart within the generator's vocabulary', partial means 'flowchart beyond it'. Story dc-2 (with dc-1) adds a new, undeclared trigger for partial: a flowchart entirely WITHIN the declared grammar still falls to partial coverage when two truth nodes' shortName collides and a proposal node becomes ambiguous. This widens the parent's ratified meaning of 'partial' from a vocabulary property of the artifact's text to also cover an identity-resolution property of the truth graph, without a supersedes/exempts edge against spec/diagram-proposals dc-3 — consumers of the coverage field per the parent's definition would misread ambiguity-induced partial as a vocabulary gap. (confidence 0.50)
